units of dental activity (nhs dentistry)#N#in the context of uk dentistry, a uda (unit of dental activity) is a measure used by nhs england to quantify dental work completed by dentists in nhs practices #N##N#ldc.org.uk#N#. dentists are allocated a certain number of udas each year, and different treatments consume different numbers of udas:#N##N#band 1 treatments (e.g., check-ups, simple cleanings) use 1 uda.#N#band 2 treatments (e.g., fillings, extractions) use 3 udas, with subdivisions:#N#band 2a: 3 udas#N#band 2b: 5 udas (non-molar endodontics or multiple teeth treatments)#N#band 2c: 7 udas (molar endodontics)#N#band 3 treatments (e.g., dentures, crowns) use 12 udas #N##N#ldc.org.uk#N#.#N#dentists report udas for each course of treatment rather than individual procedures. the uda system affects dentist remuneration, with rates varying by region, practice type, and private/nhs mix, typically ranging around £14–£15 per uda in 2025–2026 #N##N#elev8searchgroup.co.uk#N#. recent contract changes aim to improve payments for complex care, preventive treatments, and unscheduled care while gradually phasing out the uda system
